pr.title: Correct some lb related data sections to be in the correct file, split lb further
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Reorganized the early lb assembly split by deleting the monolithic asm/melee/lb/code_8000AD8C.s, moving language-related functions into a new lblanguage.s, time/saturating arithmetic helpers into a new lbtime.s, moving collision-related data and constants into lbcollision.s, and renaming/extending code_8000B1CC.s to code_8000B074.s so the 0x8000B074-0x8000B1C8 functions live with the following code range. obj_files.mk was updated to build the new lb object-file order.
what_changed: Deleted asm/melee/lb/code_8000AD8C.s, which had bundled text from 0x8000AD8C through 0x8000B1C8 plus data and sdata2 sections.
Added asm/melee/lb/lblanguage.s containing func_8000AD8C, func_8000AD98, func_8000ADB4, func_8000ADD4, func_8000ADF4, func_8000AE18, func_8000AE58, and func_8000AE90.
Added asm/melee/lb/lbtime.s containing func_8000AEC8, func_8000AEE4, func_8000AF24, func_8000AF74, func_8000AFBC, and func_8000B028.
Renamed asm/melee/lb/code_8000B1CC.s to asm/melee/lb/code_8000B074.s and prepended func_8000B074, func_8000B09C, and func_8000B134 so the file starts at 0x8000B074 instead of 0x8000B1CC.
Moved a large data block beginning at lbl_803B9928 from the deleted code_8000AD8C.s into asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s near existing collision data.
Moved sdata2 constants lbl_804D7A48, lbl_804D7A50, and lbl_804D7A54 into lbcollision.s.
Updated obj_files.mk to replace code_8000AD8C.o and code_8000B1CC.o with lblanguage.o, lbtime.o, and code_8000B074.o.
smart_moves: Preserved existing assembly bodies while relocating them, making the PR a low-risk split/relink change rather than an attempted C rewrite.
Used semantic file names for newly separated groups: lblanguage.s for language/state accessors and lbtime.s for time and clamping-style helpers.
Kept address-range comments aligned with the new file boundaries, e.g. lblanguage.s at 0x8000AD8C-0x8000B1C8, lbtime.s at 0x8000AEC8-0x8000B070, and code_8000B074.s at 0x8000B074-0x8000CE4C.
Moved collision data into lbcollision.s instead of leaving it in an address-named catchall file, improving future source-file attribution.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR, which is essential for preserving object ordering and section layout after assembly splits.
decomp_lessons: When splitting early anonymous lb assembly, do not treat address-contiguous text as necessarily one source file; use function behavior and nearby data ownership to choose target files.
Data and sdata2 ownership matters for matching: lbl_803B9928 and lbl_804D7A48/lbl_804D7A50/lbl_804D7A54 were relocated to lbcollision.s rather than kept with unrelated language/time functions.
A split can require renaming an address-based asm file to an earlier start address when functions are prepended from a previous catchall file.
For asm-only reorganization, preserve instruction text exactly and make the build-system object list reflect the intended final source/object boundaries.
Named subsystem files such as lblanguage.s and lbtime.s make later C decompilation easier than leaving ranges in code_8000XXXX.s buckets.
naming_conventions: New lb subsystem assembly files used lowercase module names with the lb prefix: lblanguage.s and lbtime.s.
Address-range catchall files retained the code_8000XXXX.s convention when the content was still not assigned a semantic name, with code_8000B1CC.s renamed to code_8000B074.s after its range changed.
Unidentified functions and labels remained in func_8000XXXX and lbl_XXXXXXXX form; this PR did not introduce higher-confidence C symbol names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Deleted a monolithic assembly file and reintroduced its functions across more precise assembly files without changing instruction bodies.
Maintained .include "macros.inc" and explicit .section declarations in each new asm file.
Preserved .global declarations for moved functions and data labels so cross-object references could still resolve.
Adjusted TEXT_O_FILES ordering in obj_files.mk to insert lblanguage.o, lbtime.o, and code_8000B074.o at the old lb position.
Kept literal constants and NULL entries in the relocated data table rather than trying to reinterpret them without stronger evidence.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review bodies were present in the slice, so there is no explicit reviewer feedback to extract.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that decompile lblanguage.s functions func_8000AD8C through func_8000AE90.
Find later PRs that decompile lbtime.s functions func_8000AEC8 through func_8000B028.
Trace lbl_803B9928 and lbl_804D7A48/lbl_804D7A50/lbl_804D7A54 to see whether later work names the collision data structures.
Check later obj_files.mk changes around asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.o, lblanguage.o, lbtime.o, and code_8000B074.o for further lb file-boundary corrections.
